seo.title: 100 Voice & Speech AI resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Voice & Speech AI resources for engineers.

content.intro: This guide provides developers with the core infrastructure, APIs, and libraries required to build production-grade Voice AI applications. It focuses on minimizing latency for real-time interaction, maximizing transcription accuracy across diverse accents, and implementing natural-sounding synthesis for conversational interfaces.

items.title: Deepgram Nova-2
items.description: Optimized for low-latency streaming transcription with specialized models for phone calls and meetings. Use the 'smart_format' parameter to automate punctuation and casing.

items.title: OpenAI Whisper Large-v3
items.description: The industry standard for accuracy in batch transcription. Implement via the 'openai-whisper' Python package or the API for multi-language support and translation.

items.title: Faster-Whisper
items.description: A reimplementation of Whisper using CTranslate2. It is up to 4x faster than the original model and uses significantly less VRAM for local hosting.

items.title: AssemblyAI LeMUR
items.description: A framework for applying LLMs to audio data. Use this to generate summaries, action items, or structured data directly from transcription outputs.

items.title: Groq Whisper (distil-whisper)
items.description: Extremely low-latency STT hosted on LPU hardware. Ideal for real-time voice agents where sub-200ms transcription is required for natural turn-taking.

items.title: Gladia API
items.description: Real-time audio intelligence layer that wraps multiple STT engines. Provides speaker diarization and sentiment analysis in a single streaming response.

items.title: Rev.ai Streaming API
items.description: High-accuracy streaming STT with support for custom vocabularies to handle industry-specific jargon or product names.

items.title: Azure Speech Services Chirp
items.description: Microsoft's foundational model for speech. Best-in-class for enterprise integration and support for over 100 languages and variants.

items.title: Speechmatics Real-Time
items.description: Focuses on 'Autonomous Speech Recognition' to reduce bias across different accents and dialects without needing fine-tuning.

items.title: Picovoice Leopard
items.description: An on-device STT engine that runs locally without cloud connectivity. Essential for privacy-first applications or offline environments.

items.title: ElevenLabs Multilingual v2
items.description: State-of-the-art neural TTS for high-fidelity, emotionally expressive voices. Use the 'latency' optimization settings for real-time streaming.

items.title: OpenAI TTS-1-HD
items.description: High-definition voice synthesis with six built-in voices. Best for cost-effective synthesis where extreme customization isn't required.

items.title: PlayHT 2.0 Turbo
items.description: An API focused on sub-250ms latency for conversational AI. Supports instant voice cloning from a 30-second audio sample.

items.title: Piper TTS
items.description: A fast, local neural TTS engine that runs on Raspberry Pi 4. Uses ONNX for inference and is ideal for edge computing and IoT.

items.title: Cartesia Sonic
items.description: A generative model designed for ultra-low latency (under 100ms). Use this for high-speed voice assistants that require immediate feedback.

items.title: Coqui TTS
items.description: A deep learning toolkit for TTS. Use this library to train your own custom voice models using datasets like LJSpeech or Common Voice.

items.title: Amazon Polly SSML
items.description: Standard cloud TTS that excels through Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) support for controlling breathing, whispering, and emphasis.

items.title: Bark by Suno AI
items.description: A transformer-based text-to-audio model that can produce non-speech sounds like laughing, sighing, and crying for more human-like output.

items.title: Google Cloud Text-to-Speech (Journey)
items.description: Neural2 and Journey models provide high-quality, studio-grade voices with a massive global footprint for localized applications.

items.title: LMNT API
items.description: Offers high-speed speech synthesis with a focus on developer experience and simple pricing for high-volume audio generation.

items.title: Silero VAD
items.description: Pre-trained Voice Activity Detector (VAD) that is highly efficient. Use this to determine when a user has started or stopped speaking to trigger STT.

items.title: LiveKit Agents
items.description: An open-source framework for building real-time AI agents. It handles the WebRTC transport and provides hooks for STT, LLM, and TTS integration.

items.title: Vapi.ai
items.description: A managed platform for voice AI agents. It orchestrates the entire stack (STT -> LLM -> TTS) and handles telephony/web integration via a single API.

items.title: Pyannote.audio
items.description: An open-source toolkit for speaker diarization. Essential for meeting transcription to identify 'who spoke when' in a multi-person conversation.

items.title: FFmpeg Audio Filters
items.description: Use 'highpass', 'lowpass', and 'loudnorm' filters to clean up user audio before sending it to STT engines to improve accuracy in noisy environments.

items.title: Web Speech API
items.description: Native browser API for simple STT and TTS. Use as a zero-cost fallback for basic voice commands without server-side processing.

items.title: Retell AI
items.description: A conversational AI API that optimizes for the 'human' feel of a conversation, including backchanneling and interruption handling.

items.title: Resemble AI (Fill)
items.description: Specialized in 'speech-to-speech' and content editing. Use to swap words in a recorded audio clip while maintaining the original speaker's voice.

items.title: Daily.co Voice SDK
items.description: WebRTC infrastructure for voice calls. Provides low-level access to audio tracks for real-time processing and AI integration.

items.title: SoX (Sound eXchange)
items.description: The 'Swiss Army knife' of audio processing. Use it for command-line audio format conversion and basic signal processing in backend pipelines.

pro_tips: Always normalize audio to 16kHz mono PCM before sending to most STT APIs; this reduces bandwidth and matches the training data of models like Whisper.
Implement Voice Activity Detection (VAD) locally on the client to avoid sending silence over the wire, significantly reducing API costs and latency.
Use Opus encoding for streaming audio to the browser; it provides the best balance between low latency and high audio quality for TTS responses.
When using LLMs for voice responses, use a streaming output and chunk the text into complete sentences before sending to the TTS engine to minimize perceived latency.
Set the 'temperature' to 0 for STT APIs to ensure deterministic transcriptions, especially when processing technical terms or proper nouns.
